source_paper_title: Rank-One Boolean Tensor Factorization and the Multilinear Polytope
source_paper_bibtex: @article{Del_Pia_2025, title={Rank-One Boolean Tensor Factorization and the Multilinear Polytope}, volume={50}, ISSN={1526-5471}, url={http://dx.doi.org/10.1287/moor.2022.0201}, DOI={10.1287/moor.2022.0201}, number={2}, journal={Mathematics of Operations Research}, publisher={Institute for Operations Research and the Management Sciences (INFORMS)}, author={Del Pia, Alberto and Khajavirad, Aida}, year={2025}, month=may, pages={1514–1554} }
context:
Page 4, Section 1.4 (Our contribution).

Numerical experiments suggest that our recovery guarantees are fairly tight, and that the complete LP significantly outperforms the flower LP. We believe that obtaining recovery guarantees for the complete LP is an interesting open question.
title: Recovery threshold for complete LP relaxation in semi-random rank-one Boolean tensors
background_latex:
Let \(n,m,l\in\mathbb{Z}_{\ge 1}\) and let \(G\in\{0,1\}^{n\times m\times l}\) be an observed binary tensor. A rank-one binary (Boolean) tensor is a tensor of the form
\[W=x\otimes y\otimes z,\qquad x\in\{0,1\}^n,\ y\in\{0,1\}^m,\ z\in\{0,1\}^l,\]
with entries \(w_{ijk}=x_i y_j z_k\).

Assume a planted (ground-truth) rank-one tensor \(\bar W=\bar x\otimes \bar y\otimes \bar z\in\{0,1\}^{n\times m\times l}\). In the fully-random corruption model with parameter \(p\in[0,1]\), each entry is independently flipped: \(g_{ijk}=\bar w_{ijk}\) with probability \(1-p\) and \(g_{ijk}=1-\bar w_{ijk}\) with probability \(p\). In the associated semi-random corruption model, after the fully-random flips an adversary may apply any sequence of monotone corrections: it may change \(g_{ijk}\) from \(0\) to \(1\) only when \(\bar w_{ijk}=1\), and from \(1\) to \(0\) only when \(\bar w_{ijk}=0\).

An optimization problem is said to recover the ground truth if it has a unique optimal solution and that solution corresponds to \(\bar W\) (equivalently \((\bar x,\bar y,\bar z)\)).

The paper studies linear programming relaxations of rank-one Boolean tensor factorization. The strongest relaxation considered is the \(\textit{complete LP}\), which optimizes the standard linearized objective for rank-one BTF using an extended formulation with auxiliary variables representing all bilinear products and constraints capturing the convex hull of the local multilinear relations.

Concretely, define index sets from the observed tensor \(G\):
\[S_0:=\{(i,j,k): g_{ijk}=0\},\qquad S_1:=\{(i,j,k): g_{ijk}=1\}.\]
Introduce variables \(x\in[0,1]^n\), \(y\in[0,1]^m\), \(z\in[0,1]^l\), and auxiliary variables \(w\in[0,1]^{n\times m\times l}\) as well as bilinear auxiliaries \(w^1\in[0,1]^{n\times m}\), \(w^2\in[0,1]^{n\times l}\), \(w^3\in[0,1]^{m\times l}\). The complete LP minimizes
\[\sum_{(i,j,k)\in S_0} w_{ijk}+\sum_{(i,j,k)\in S_1}(1-w_{ijk}),\]
subject to a system of linear inequalities (given explicitly in the paper) that enforce a tight convex relaxation of
\(w_{ijk}=x_i y_j z_k\) together with \(w^1_{ij}=x_i y_j\), \(w^2_{ik}=x_i z_k\), \(w^3_{jk}=y_j z_k\), in a way implied by level-2 reformulation-linearization but with only \(O(nml)\) constraints.

Let the planted factor densities be
\[r_{\bar x}:=\frac{1}{n}\sum_{i=1}^n \bar x_i,\quad r_{\bar y}:=\frac{1}{m}\sum_{j=1}^m \bar y_j,\quad r_{\bar z}:=\frac{1}{l}\sum_{k=1}^l \bar z_k,\quad r_{\bar W}:=r_{\bar x} r_{\bar y} r_{\bar z}.\]
The paper establishes high-probability recovery thresholds for weaker LPs (standard LP and flower LP) under the semi-random model, but does not give such a threshold for the complete LP.
problem_definition_latex:
Determine (in the semi-random corruption model for planted rank-one binary tensors) a high-probability recovery guarantee for the complete LP relaxation.

More explicitly: characterize, in terms of the corruption probability \(p\) and planted densities \(r_{\bar x},r_{\bar y},r_{\bar z}\) (and asymptotic growth of \(n,m,l\)), conditions under which the complete LP has a unique optimal solution corresponding to the planted tensor \(\bar W=\bar x\otimes \bar y\otimes \bar z\) with probability tending to one as \(n,m,l\to\infty\).
latex:
Let \(n,m,l\in\mathbb{Z}_{\ge 1}\) and let \(G\in\{0,1\}^{n\times m\times l}\) be an observed binary tensor. A rank-one binary (Boolean) tensor is a tensor of the form
\[W=x\otimes y\otimes z,\qquad x\in\{0,1\}^n,\ y\in\{0,1\}^m,\ z\in\{0,1\}^l,\]
with entries \(w_{ijk}=x_i y_j z_k\).

Assume a planted (ground-truth) rank-one tensor \(\bar W=\bar x\otimes \bar y\otimes \bar z\in\{0,1\}^{n\times m\times l}\). In the fully-random corruption model with parameter \(p\in[0,1]\), each entry is independently flipped: \(g_{ijk}=\bar w_{ijk}\) with probability \(1-p\) and \(g_{ijk}=1-\bar w_{ijk}\) with probability \(p\). An optimization problem is said to recover the ground truth if it has a unique optimal solution and that solution corresponds to \(\bar W\) (equivalently \((\bar x,\bar y,\bar z)\)).

The paper studies linear programming relaxations of rank-one Boolean tensor factorization. The strongest relaxation considered is the \(\textit{complete LP}\), which optimizes the standard linearized objective for rank-one BTF using an extended formulation with auxiliary variables representing all bilinear products and constraints capturing the convex hull of the local multilinear relations.

Let the planted factor densities be
\[r_{\bar x}:=\frac{1}{n}\sum_{i=1}^n \bar x_i,\quad r_{\bar y}:=\frac{1}{m}\sum_{j=1}^m \bar y_j,\quad r_{\bar z}:=\frac{1}{l}\sum_{k=1}^l \bar z_k,\quad r_{\bar W}:=r_{\bar x} r_{\bar y} r_{\bar z}.\]

Determine (in the semi-random corruption model for planted rank-one binary tensors) a high-probability recovery guarantee for the complete LP relaxation.

More explicitly: characterize, in terms of the corruption probability \(p\) and planted densities \(r_{\bar x},r_{\bar y},r_{\bar z}\) (and asymptotic growth of \(n,m,l\)), conditions under which the complete LP has a unique optimal solution corresponding to the planted tensor \(\bar W=\bar x\otimes \bar y\otimes \bar z\) with probability tending to one as \(n,m,l\to\infty\).
open_problem_quote: Numerical experiments suggest that our recovery guarantees are fairly tight, and that the complete LP significantly outperforms the flower LP. We believe that obtaining recovery guarantees for the complete LP is an interesting open question.
counterexample_suitability_rationale: One can generate many random planted instances (and semi-random monotone adversarial modifications) and empirically test whether the complete LP recovers \(\bar W\), making automated search for failure instances feasible. However, the adversary space is enormous, and certifying a true worst-case semi-random counterexample may require nontrivial optimization over monotone transformations.
alphaevolve_suitability_rationale: Candidate statements (e.g., conjectured thresholds or finite-size scaling laws) can be scored by running the complete LP on large batches of simulated planted instances and estimating recovery probability as a function of \(p\) and densities. An evolutionary loop can search over functional forms/parameters for conjectured bounds or over adversarial monotone modifications that degrade recovery, using recovery rate as an automatic fitness signal.

verification.notice: Generated from the paper text only and not independently verified as still open.

literature_review.synthesis:
No forward-citation evidence was provided, so there is currently no identified paper that resolves the open problem of a sharp high-probability recovery threshold for the complete LP (cLP) in the semi-random corruption model for planted rank-one Boolean tensors. The source paper establishes that the NP-hard maximum-likelihood formulation recovers w.h.p. iff \(p<1/2\) (under mild growth assumptions), and proves sufficient semi-random recovery thresholds for two weaker LP relaxations: the standard LP (sLP) and the flower LP (fLP). In the dense regime with tensor density \(r_{\bar W}=r_{\bar x}r_{\bar y}r_{\bar z}\), the flower LP succeeds for \(p< r_{\bar W}/(1+2r_{\bar W})\), while numerical experiments indicate that the complete LP significantly outperforms the flower LP.

The complete LP strengthens the standard LP by introducing bilinear auxiliaries \(w^1,w^2,w^3\) and enforcing local convex-hull constraints equivalent to a compact subset of level-2 RLT constraints; it also implicitly contains flower and running-intersection inequalities. A plausible route to a recovery guarantee is to extend the dual-certificate approach used for sLP/fLP: construct dual variables for constraints (9)–(12) that certify optimality of \((\bar x,\bar y,\bar z,\bar W)\), then prove strict feasibility/uniqueness via a Mangasarian-type argument. The main technical gap is to exploit the additional coupling provided by \(w^1,w^2,w^3\) to relax the per-index concentration requirements that limit the flower LP threshold, potentially pushing the provable threshold closer to the information-theoretic limit \(p<1/2\) (at least in dense regimes). At present, however, this remains open.

sources.title: Rank-one Boolean tensor factorization and the multilinear polytope
sources.theorem_or_result:
Introduces the complete LP (cLP) for rank-one Boolean tensor factorization using an extended formulation capturing local convex hull constraints for 
\(w_{ijk}=x_i y_j z_k\) together with bilinear auxiliaries \(w^1,w^2,w^3\). Proves high-probability recovery thresholds under a semi-random corruption model for the standard LP and the flower LP, but leaves the recovery threshold for the complete LP as an open question.
sources.connection: This is the source paper posing the open problem: obtain a high-probability recovery guarantee for the complete LP under the semi-random corruption model, improving upon the flower LP threshold \(p< r_{\bar W}/(1+2r_{\bar W})\).

sources.title: Uniqueness of solution in linear programming
sources.theorem_or_result: Gives a characterization of uniqueness of LP optimal solutions via active constraints and dual variables: uniqueness holds if there is no nonzero direction satisfying \(C_K x=0,\ C_L x\le 0\) for the sets of tight constraints with positive/zero dual multipliers.
sources.connection: The source paper uses Mangasarian’s criterion to prove uniqueness for the standard and flower LPs. Any complete-LP recovery proof will likely need an analogous dual certificate plus uniqueness argument, so this is methodological background.

sources.title: A hierarchy of relaxations between the continuous and convex hull representations for zero-one programming problems
sources.theorem_or_result: Introduces the Reformulation-Linearization Technique (RLT) hierarchy; level-2 RLT yields linear relaxations for multilinear 0-1 programs by adding product variables and linear constraints approximating the convex hull.
sources.connection: The complete LP is described as being contained in level-2 RLT for the rank-one BTF formulation but with only \(O(nml)\) constraints. Understanding RLT and convex-hull descriptions is relevant for analyzing tightness and potential recovery thresholds of the complete LP.

sources.title: Concave extensions for non-linear 0−1 maximization problems
sources.theorem_or_result: Studies convex/concave envelopes and linearization techniques for nonlinear 0-1 optimization, including tight relaxations for multilinear terms over the hypercube.
sources.connection: The standard LP uses convex-hull linearizations of \(w_{ijk}=x_i y_j z_k\) over \([0,1]^3\) and objective-sign-based constraint pruning. Envelope results underpin the relaxation strength comparisons relevant to complete LP analysis.

sources.title: The multilinear polytope for acyclic hypergraphs
sources.theorem_or_result: Develops flower inequalities and shows they yield compact extended formulations for multilinear polytopes associated with acyclic hypergraphs; provides facet/validity results for these inequalities.
sources.connection: Flower inequalities are used to define the flower LP, which is implied by the complete LP. Techniques for deriving and analyzing such inequalities inform how one might build dual certificates for the complete LP and potentially sharpen thresholds.

sources.title: The running intersection relaxation of the multilinear polytope
sources.theorem_or_result: Introduces running intersection inequalities/relaxations for multilinear polytopes; proves strength and extended formulation properties, and relates them to decomposability of multilinear sets.
sources.connection: The complete LP constraints (10)–(12) are described as running intersection inequalities for an augmented hypergraph. Understanding these inequalities is central to analyzing why the complete LP empirically outperforms the flower LP and what additional structure might yield better recovery thresholds.

sources.title: Heuristics for semirandom graph problems
sources.theorem_or_result: Introduces semi-random (random + adversarial monotone) models for combinatorial problems and analyzes robustness of algorithms under such models.
sources.connection: The corruption model in the source paper is a tensor analogue of semi-random models. Conceptual tools from semi-random analysis motivate robustness arguments (e.g., Proposition 2) that reduce semi-random recovery to fully-random recovery for monotone objectives.
